title: Developmental cell programs are co-opted in inflammatory skin disease
snippets: Melanocytes are among the stromal cell types identified in the adult human skin atlas, alongside fibroblasts, endothelial cells, pericytes, and Schwann cells

title: Single-cell transcriptomic atlas of human skin identifies melanocyte heterogeneity and regulatory networks
snippets: PMEL, MITF, MLANA, TYRP1, DCT are defining markers of melanocytes in single-cell RNA-seq studies of human skin
Melanocytes cluster together and are readily distinguished from other skin cell types by the co-expression of TYRP1, DCT, MLANA, and PMEL

title: Melanocyte biology and skin pigmentation
snippets: MITF is essential for melanocyte survival, proliferation, and differentiation, and directly activates transcription of melanin synthesis genes TYR, TYRP1 and DCT
